system.name: DS Contracts POC
system.source: Compiled from contracts/*.contract.json + tokens/ + context/rules.json — do not edit.

package.name: ds-contracts-poc
package.usage: import { Button, Card, Table } from 'ds-contracts-poc'; import 'ds-contracts-poc/styles.css';

rules.statement: Every UI element comes from the catalog. Unknown components are gaps to report, never to invent.
No literal colors or pixel values anywhere. All styling flows through design tokens (var(--…)) — and in screens, styling flows only through components.
Never use raw HTML elements the system provides a component for.
No className or style props on system components. If a component can't express it, that's a gap in the contract — report it.
At most one primary Button per screen.
Compose layout with Stack and Inline, not hand-styled wrappers.
Avatars represent people or entities, never decoration.

context.memory:
# System memory

What this design system is and is for — the standing context every generating
surface reads before producing anything. Edit freely (or via Contract Hub's
Context page); this ships inside the catalog.

- This system serves data-dense B2B product surfaces: settings, directories,
  billing, member management.
- Tone: quiet and information-first. Color signals state, never decoration.
- Density matters: tables and forms should hold up at compact rhythm.
- When a request needs something the catalog lacks, report the gap — never
  approximate with lookalikes.
context.guidance: Memory is standing intent; rules are the constitution (judge-enforced where deterministic); references are consultable files for judgment calls. Deterministic checks always win over interpretation.

tokens.guidance: Screens should not need tokens directly — styling flows through components. Where a token is unavoidable, use var(--…) with a name from this list, preferring semantic names.

components.name: AccordionItem
components.description: A collapsible content row: trigger with a state chevron, content revealed when open. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Collapsible) with the open state flattened to a closed/open enum so both surfaces render both states; the toggle itself is contract-declared (onToggle + aria-expanded, generated); richer behavior stays a declared boundary.

props.name: state
props.description: Closed shows only the trigger; open reveals the content and rotates the chevron.

events.description: Fires when the trigger is activated; uncontrolled instances flip state closed/open themselves.

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Represents a person or entity with initials. Non-interactive.

components.name: AvatarGroup
components.description: Stacked avatars with an overflow count. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx AvatarGroup): overlapping layout — expressed as a negative-margin child rule in CSS and negative item spacing on the canvas.

components.name: Badge
components.description: Communicates status or categorization at a glance. Non-interactive.

components.name: Button
components.description: Triggers an action or event. Use one primary button per context.

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows a spinning busy indicator beside the label while an async action resolves.

components.name: Banner
components.description: Persistent inline notification for page- or section-level feedback. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Banner): status drives color, icon, and ARIA role; dismissable banners expose a close affordance; endContent carries actions.

props.name: status
props.description: Feedback tone — drives the color scheme, the leading icon, and the ARIA role (error/warning announce as alerts).

props.name: container
props.description: card = standalone with radius; section = full-bleed, square.

props.name: isDismissable
props.description: Shows the close affordance. Keep error banners until resolved.

props.name: description
props.default: Supporting detail that explains what happened and what to do next.

slots.description: End-aligned action slot — typically a secondary Button.

components.name: Blockquote
components.description: Styled quotation block with an accent left border for quotes, testimonials, and excerpts. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Blockquote): content plus an optional cite slot rendered in semantic footer/cite markup.

slots.description: Attribution, rendered in a semantic footer.

components.name: BreadcrumbItem
components.description: One link in a breadcrumb trail with its leading separator. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx BreadcrumbItem); the first item is authored with hasSeparator off (positional part logic is a documented gap), and aria-current needs conditional attributes — also documented.

props.name: hasSeparator
props.description: Leading separator — off for the first item in a trail.

props.name: href
props.description: Navigation target. Current-page items conventionally point at themselves.

components.name: Breadcrumbs
components.description: Navigation trail from root to the current page. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Breadcrumbs): a nav landmark holding BreadcrumbItems; keep trails to five levels or fewer.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the navigation landmark.

components.name: Card
components.description: Groups related content behind one subject. Composes an Avatar, a bound title, a default body slot, and a constrained actions slot.

props.name: title
props.description: Card heading, bound to the header title part on both surfaces.

children.description: Default slot — the card's main content.

slots.description: Constrained actions slot — only actions-grade components.

components.name: ChatMessageMetadata
components.description: Metadata row for a chat message: delivery status, timestamp, and footer content. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx ChatMessageMetadata): status drives the icon.

props.name: status
props.description: Delivery status — drives the leading icon.

slots.description: Footer content — model info, reactions, a copy affordance.

components.name: ChatMessage
components.description: Sender-context wrapper for a chat message: avatar, name, bubble content, and metadata. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx ChatMessage); the bubble color follows sender, and sender-based alignment flips via layoutByProp — user messages render right-aligned (row-reverse root, end-aligned body) on both surfaces.

props.name: sender
props.description: Who sent it — drives the bubble color scheme.

props.name: name
props.description: Sender name shown above the message body.

slots.description: Avatar beside the message.
Timestamp and delivery status below the bubble.

components.name: ChatSystemMessage
components.description: Centered system message for non-sender chat content — date separators, membership changes, status notices. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx ChatSystemMessage): the divider variant adds a line on each side.

props.name: variant
props.description: Plain centered text, or text between divider lines.

props.name: message
props.description: Short factual string — a date, a join notice, a status change.

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Toggles a single on/off value — settings, terms acceptance, opt-ins. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx CheckboxInput): checked, unchecked, and indeterminate are prop-driven appearance states.

props.name: value
props.description: Checked, unchecked, or indeterminate (partial selection in a group).

props.name: label
props.description: Always rendered — users must know what they are toggling.

events.description: Fires when the box is activated; uncontrolled instances flip unchecked/checked (indeterminate resolves to checked).

components.name: Citation
components.description: Inline reference to an external source — attribution in AI-generated responses, articles, anywhere provenance matters. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Citation): label chips or compact numbered badges.

props.name: variant
props.description: Label chip with the source title, or a compact numbered badge. Don't mix both in one paragraph.

components.name: Code
components.description: Inline code element with monospace font and muted background. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Code); block code is a separate component family.

components.name: Divider
components.description: Visual separator dividing content into distinct sections. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Divider) at the variant level; orientation requires per-enum-value property overrides — a documented schema gap.

components.name: EmptyState
components.description: Placeholder for a content area with no data — empty lists, zero results, first-time setup. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx EmptyState): always a title and a next step; the icon is decorative.

props.name: description
props.default: When items are added they will show up in this list.
props.description: Secondary text explaining what will appear here and how.

slots.description: Decorative icon or illustration above the title.
Action buttons below the description.

components.name: Field
components.description: Form field wrapper providing a label, description, and required indicator around any control. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Field): the label targets the control via inputID; validation status needs structured-object props — a documented gap.

props.name: isRequired
props.description: Shows the required indicator beside the label.

props.name: label
props.description: Always rendered — even custom controls need names.

props.name: inputID
props.description: The wrapped control's id, used for the label's htmlFor association.

props.name: description
props.default: Helper text for the wrapped control.
props.description: Helper text between the label and the control.

components.name: Heading
components.description: Sectioning heading whose rendered HTML element follows the level prop (h1–h6) via semantics.elementByProp, with a visual size ramp on the existing control type scale — document outline and visual weight are deliberately independent axes. Text nodes carry no element semantics, so only code renders the element itself.

props.name: level
props.description: Document outline level — drives the rendered element (h1–h6) and the size ramp.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size on the control type scale — independent of the document outline level.

components.name: IconButton
components.description: A button showing only an icon, for toolbars, table rows, and compact UI. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx IconButton): the required label is the accessible name and is never rendered visibly.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style. Ghost sits on the surface wash (transparent tokens are a documented gap).

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name (aria-label). Be specific: "Delete conversation", not "Delete".

slots.description: The icon. Pass an Icon-sized element; it inherits the variant's foreground color.

components.name: Inline
components.description: Horizontal layout primitive — lays children out in a row with a token-governed gap, vertically centered.

components.name: Kbd
components.description: Renders a keyboard shortcut as a styled key badge for tooltips, menus, and help text. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Kbd); multi-key parsing ("mod+K" into separate badges) is display logic — a documented gap, one badge per component for now.

components.name: ListItem
components.description: A single row in a List: label with optional description, leading and trailing content. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx ListItem); click/link behavior is a declared boundary.

slots.description: Leading content — icon or avatar.
Trailing content — badge, count, chevron.

components.name: List
components.description: Vertical collection of items with consistent density. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx List); dividers need sibling-selector styling and marker styles need list-style tokens — documented gaps.

components.name: MetadataListItem
components.description: One key–value pair in a MetadataList: a label and a value area. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx MetadataListItem).

props.name: value
props.description: The value text. Use the children slot instead for rich values.

slots.description: Icon before the label.

components.name: MetadataList
components.description: Key–value pairs for object attributes — detail panels, settings summaries, record information. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx MetadataList); multi-column layout and collapse behavior are documented boundaries.

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Steps through pages of content, below a table or list. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Pagination) with three display variants; page math and click behavior are declared boundaries — the pages variant shows a representative trail.

props.name: variant
props.description: Numbered trail, compact page label, or dot indicators.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the navigation landmark.

components.name: ProgressBar
components.description: Horizontal bar showing completion progress. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx ProgressBar): number-valued value/max drive the fill; the canvas renders the defaults' fraction as its honest static state. Indeterminate mode needs animation on the canvas — a documented boundary.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name — screen readers must know what is progressing.

components.name: Section
components.description: The standard way to create page regions and group related content — settings groups, form sections, sidebar areas. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Section) at the variant level; the transparent variant needs transparent color tokens — a documented gap.

props.name: variant
props.description: Background treatment. Muted only to call attention.

components.name: SideNavItem
components.description: A sidebar navigation row: icon, label, and trailing content, with a selected state. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx SideNavItem); nesting and collapse are behavior-layer boundaries.

props.name: state
props.description: Whether this item is the current page.

slots.description: Trailing badge or count.

components.name: Skeleton
components.description: Pulsing placeholder previewing the shape of loading content. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Skeleton) with the free-form width/height flattened to a shape variant; the pulse is CSS-only — the canvas shows the static shape.

components.name: Slider
components.description: Numeric selection within bounds — the static surface of a slider: label, filled track to the current value, and thumb. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Slider); drag behavior and range mode are declared boundaries.

components.name: Spinner
components.description: Animated loading indicator for processes of unknown duration. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Spinner); the size scale needs per-variant icon sizing and the rotation itself is CSS-only — the canvas shows the static arc.

props.name: label
props.description: What is loading — announced to screen readers.

components.name: Stack
components.description: Vertical layout primitive — stacks children with a token-governed gap. Screens compose from Stack/Inline instead of raw styled divs.

components.name: StatusDot
components.description: Small colored dot communicating status — presence, severity, activity. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx StatusDot): five semantic variants with a required accessible label.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label announced by screen readers — status must never be conveyed by color alone.

components.name: Switch
components.description: Toggle for on/off settings that take effect immediately. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Switch) with the boolean value flattened to an off/on enum so both surfaces render both states truthfully; toggle behavior is a declared boundary.

props.name: value
props.description: On or off — drives the track color and thumb position.

props.name: label
props.description: Always rendered — users must know what they are toggling.

events.description: Fires when the track is activated; uncontrolled instances flip value off/on themselves.

components.name: Tab
components.description: A single tab in a TabList. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Tab) with selection flattened to a state enum so both surfaces render it truthfully; selection behavior itself is a declared boundary.

props.name: state
props.description: Selection state — selected tabs render semibold in the accent color.

slots.description: Badge count or status dot after the label.

components.name: TabList
components.description: Tab-style navigation container holding Tab items. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TabList); roving-tabindex keyboard behavior and controlled selection are declared boundaries.

components.name: TableCell
components.description: A single data cell in a table row.

props.name: density
props.description: Vertical rhythm, normally driven by the owning Table.

components.name: TableHeaderCell
components.description: A column header cell.

props.name: density
props.description: Vertical rhythm, normally driven by the owning Table.

components.name: TableRow
components.description: A selectable row of table cells.

children.description: The row's cells. Multi-child slot: renders its default content directly in Figma (INSTANCE_SWAP cannot hold multiple children — see docs/08).

components.name: Table
components.description: Structured data in rows and columns. Composes header cells (with the Table's density mapped into them), and a body slot of rows.

props.name: density
props.description: Vertical rhythm of the whole table; mapped into the fixed header cells.

children.description: Rows. Multi-child slot: renders its default content directly in Figma (see docs/08).

components.name: TextArea
components.description: Multi-line text input for longer-form content — comments, descriptions, messages. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TextArea); wrapping-label anatomy gives implicit association.

props.name: size
props.description: Control size scale (affects padding and text size).

props.name: description
props.default: Helper text that explains the expected content.
props.description: Helper text between the label and the textarea.

components.name: TextField
components.description: Single-line text input for short-form values — names, emails, search queries. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TextInput): required label, description, size scale. The wrapping-label anatomy gives implicit label association. Disabled fields dim and drop pointer interaction via stylesWhen (code-side conditional styling; a declared canvas fidelity limit).

props.name: isRequired
props.description: Marks the field required — shows the indicator beside the label.

props.name: label
props.description: Field label — always rendered; placeholders are not labels.

props.name: description
props.default: Helper text that explains the expected value.
props.description: Helper text between the label and the input.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Hint shown when the field is empty. Never a substitute for the label.

components.name: Toast
components.description: Brief non-blocking notification confirming an action. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Toast): the visual element — positioning, stacking, and auto-dismiss belong to the behavior layer and are a declared boundary.

props.name: type
props.description: Info confirms; error persists until dismissed (behavior layer).

slots.description: Trailing content — an Undo button, a link.

components.name: Token
components.description: Small inline element for discrete associated data — tags, categories, active filters. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Token): 11-color vocabulary with size scale; removal and click behavior are a declared boundary.

slots.description: Icon before the label.
Content after the label — a count Badge, a chevron.

components.name: Toolbar
components.description: General-purpose toolbar container with start, center, and end content areas for contextual actions. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx Toolbar); roving-tabindex keyboard behavior is a declared boundary.

props.name: size
props.description: Minimum toolbar height; children keep their own sizes.

slots.description: Start-aligned actions — buttons, segmented controls, selectors.
Centered content.
End-aligned primary actions, selectors, badges.

components.name: TopNavItem
components.description: A navigation item in a TopNav or SideNav context. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TopNavItem) with selection flattened to a state enum; the selected state applies aria-current.

props.name: state
props.description: Whether this item is the current page.

components.name: TopNav
components.description: Horizontal main navigation bar with heading, start, center, and end areas. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TopNav); hover menus and mega-menus are behavior-layer boundaries.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the navigation landmark.

slots.description: Logo or brand at the start edge.
Primary navigation items after the heading.
Search, icons, user profile at the end edge.

components.name: TypeaheadItem
components.description: Default dropdown item for search results: label with optional icon and description. API mirrors industry convention (Astryx TypeaheadItem) with the item object flattened to explicit props.

slots.description: Icon or avatar before the label.
